2606.29090.title: AB-RAG: Adaptive Budgeted Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Reliable Question Answering
2606.29090.paper_summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has become the standard way to ground large language models in external knowledge, yet most systems retrieve a fixed number of passages for every question regardless of its difficulty. This wastes computation on easy questions, starves hard ones, and gives no signal for when a generated answer can be trusted. With a growing share of question answering systems built on top of commercial language model APIs, a method that can decide how much to retrieve, and how far to trust its own answers, without retraining the underlying model, is of clear practical value. This paper presents AB-RAG (Adaptive Budgeted Retrieval-Augmented Generation), a training-free and backbone-agnostic framework that generates an answer, estimates its confidence from a combination of three signals, and then decides whether to stop or to retrieve more evidence, subject to a fixed retrieval budget. The estimator combines the model's own certainty, the agreement between the answer and the evidence, and the variance of the retrieval scores. For models that expose token probabilities the certainty signal is read directly; for closed APIs it is approximated by self-consistency, so the method works without access to model internals. Across three backbones and two datasets, the central result is that the confidence estimate reliably separates correct from incorrect answers on every backbone, reaching a clean split of 57.6% against 0% Exact Match between high- and low-confidence answers on a factoid dataset. The adaptive policy improves accuracy on capable backbones, and the study reports its negative and nuanced findings honestly, including a confidence signal that proved unsuitable for short answers and a retrieval signal whose sign was found and corrected through measurement. The entire study was carried out on a single consumer laptop with only a few dollars of API spend.
2606.29090.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）已成為將大型語言模型與外部知識結合的標準方式，然而大多數系統對於每個問題檢索的段落數量是固定的，無論其難度如何。這在簡單問題上浪費了計算資源，對於困難問題則無法提供足夠的支持，並且沒有任何信號表明生成的答案何時可以被信任。隨著越來越多的問答系統建立在商業語言模型API之上，一種能夠決定檢索多少資料，以及在多大程度上信任自身答案的方法，而無需重新訓練基礎模型，顯然具有實際價值。本文提出了AB-RAG（自適應預算檢索增強生成），這是一個無需訓練且與骨幹無關的框架，能夠生成答案，從三個信號的組合中估計其信心，然後決定是否停止或檢索更多證據，受限於固定的檢索預算。該估計器結合了模型自身的確定性、答案與證據之間的一致性以及檢索分數的變異性。對於暴露出標記概率的模型，確定性信號是直接讀取的；對於封閉API，則通過自一致性來近似，因此該方法在無需訪問模型內部的情況下也能運作。在三個骨幹和兩個數據集上，核心結果是信心估計能可靠地區分正確答案和錯誤答案，在每個骨幹上，達到57.6%對0%的精確匹配的清晰分割，針對高信心和低信心答案的事實數據集。自適應策略提高了在有能力的骨幹上的準確性，研究如實報告了其負面和細緻的發現，包括一個對短答案不適用的信心信號，以及一個通過測量發現並修正其符號的檢索信號。整個研究是在一台普通消費者筆記本電腦上進行的，API支出僅幾美元。
2606.29090.author: Ansh Kamthan et.al.
